verbatim: [x] Exclude explicit empty-model summary points from Funnel A.

verbatim: [x] Refuse new summary writes without explicit model provenance.

No dated receipt on the ledger line; a one-shot negative-control assert closes it per the assert-before-commit discipline.

verbatim: [x] Count only summaries actually accepted by the Qdrant writer.

Consistency between script totals and a raw Qdrant scroll count is the remaining spot-check.

verbatim: [ ] Verify every valid summary has child IDs, boundaries, model, schema, validation status, and evidence-backed semantics.
action: Extend p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify with a per-summary field audit: source_child_ids non-empty and resolvable to Mongo chunks rows; child_boundaries provenance present; summary_model non-empty; schema_version present; validation status present; semantics fields (key_terms/mechanisms/concept_tags, and post-S4 latent_concepts/temporal_class) evidence-backed. Run it AFTER the S4 consolidated pass so the pair's back-level rows are not certified prematurely.
who: junior-agent builds the verify extension; script executes it
where: backend/scripts/p0_1_summary_integrity.py; Mongo parent_chunks (source_child_ids, summary fields); Qdrant summary payloads
why: A summary without child provenance cannot be trusted as hydration evidence and cannot be audited for hallucination.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY WARNING (registry_hierarchy 'boundaries', verdict DISTINCT): 'boundaries' here means Ghost A child_boundaries / durable source_child_ids provenance on parent rows — NOT summary_tree section_range (that is P0.2 L391's sense). One validator for both would be wrong. Also 'child IDs' means source_child_ids (chunk ids), not tree child_node_ids. Sequencing: certifying 'evidence-backed semantics' before S4 would violate the spirit of Standing Rule 1 — the pair's semantics fields are known back-level (PLAN_CRITIQUE C1). Base fields (IDs/model/schema) could be spot-checked earlier as a gap-filler, but the closing run belongs after S4.
required_receipt: Extended verify run, exit 0, reporting per-corpus: 100% summaries with resolvable source_child_ids, model, schema_version, validation status; 0 rows failing the semantics-evidence check; dated restamp on the ledger line.

verbatim: [ ] Verify document-summary trees roll up only validated children.
action: Build a rollup-provenance validator: for every summary_tree rollup node, assert all member parent_ids reference parents whose summaries are validated (non-empty model + validation status), and that no rollup includes quarantined/superseded parents. Run after S4 (pair summaries regenerate there) and after any backfill_tree_concepts re-run.
where: Mongo summary_tree (TreeNode.parent_ids per registry_hierarchy); Mongo parent_chunks; backend/services/ingestion/summary_tree.py
why: Rollups built over invalid children launder placeholder content into the tree-descent retrieval path.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY WARNING x2 (registry_hierarchy): (1) 'document-summary trees' here = the per-doc summary_tree (canonical summary_tree), NOT doc_profile/Tier-0 doc_summaries cards — three referents share 'doc summary'. (2) On TreeNode, parent_ids = L1 MEMBER parent-chunk ids and child_node_ids = tree children; 'roll up only validated children' means validating parent_ids members, despite the word 'children'. A validator keyed on child_node_ids would invert the hierarchy. Feasible now mechanically, but a pre-S4 pass would need re-running; slot with S4's verify.
required_receipt: Validator run, exit 0: 0 rollup nodes referencing unvalidated/superseded parents, per corpus; counts logged per node_type.

ALSO a word collision: strict_ready is simultaneously the top value of the temporal capability enum (temporal_unavailable|partial|strict_ready) — this acceptance means the corpus publication gate, not the temporal tier. As written the acceptance was verified against 'all four corpora' as a stand-in for a gate that doesn't exist yet; S6 must restate it.

verbatim: [ ] Funnel A returns no byte-identical parent replacement presented as a summary.
action: Build the probe: sample Funnel A results across the four corpora and assert no returned summary text is byte-identical to its parent chunk text (compare Qdrant summary payload text vs Mongo parent_chunks text for every sampled hit); wire it as an asserting script with non-zero exit on any match.
where: backend/services/retriever/funnel_a.py (read path); Qdrant summary points; Mongo parent_chunks
why: A byte-identical 'summary' is an extractive fallback masquerading as abstraction — the exact fraud class P0.1 exists to kill.
feasibility_note: Independent of the S4 capture fields — it tests text identity, not semantics — so it is runnable immediately as a gap-filler (plan §3 parallelizable clause covers P0.2/P0.6 remainders; this P0.1 probe is equally non-blocking). With 0 empty-model rows remaining (V), a residual byte-identical case would come from a model echoing input, which only a live probe catches.
required_receipt: Probe run over N>=500 sampled Funnel A hits per corpus: 0 byte-identical summary/parent pairs; exit 0; ledger restamp with date + sample size.

verbatim: [ ] Fast, Hybrid, and Graph recall do not regress after placeholder removal.

Final closure rides the S14 full regression matrix.

Baseline exists (task ledger: baseline captured; plan S8 references 'the 2026-07-13 baseline'). Caveat: the CURRENT deployed latency baseline shows Fast degraded at 39.8s (checklist Current Durable Baseline) — recall comparison is still valid, but interleave with S0's warmup fixes to avoid conflating latency noise. Can run early as a gap-filler; S14 is the binding gate.

Decision must land before any reingest lane (S3 outcome / S11 burst) builds new trees at scale.

where: backend/services/ingestion/summary_tree.py (no omit/alias branch exists today); docs/RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md P0.2

Canonical term: 'passthrough section (one-child section)'; avoid 'singleton' in the implementation (collides with service-instance singletons). Pairing with S3 is natural: S3 already convenes the owner for the per-corpus reingest disposition matrix, and reingest is exactly when new-tree policy takes effect. L390's measurements are the decision's evidence input — sequence L390 first.

All inputs live: 21,432 sections / 20,942 one-child / 23,394 rollups baseline for cross-checking, navigator lane diagnostics already label descent modes (registry_hierarchy 'hierarchy descent'). Read-only; no freeze conflict.

verbatim: [ ] Validate document profile, section, rollup, parent, and child boundaries on PDF, transcript, Markdown, HTML, and structured-list fixtures.
action: Build the fixture suite: one small document per format (PDF, transcript, Markdown, HTML, structured-list) ingested through the real pipeline in a test corpus; assert document profile node, section grouping, rollup membership (parent_ids), parent extents, and child extents match golden expectations. House under backend/tests with non-zero exit on fail.
where: backend/tests (new fixtures); backend/services/ingestion/{docling_adapter.py,tier_chunker.py,summary_tree.py}; disposable test corpus
why: Boundary correctness across formats is the only guard that new-format ingests build sane trees before paid passes run over them.
feasibility_note: Gap-filler per plan §3, and quietly load-bearing for S3/S11 (a reingest lane without fixture coverage re-buys risk). TERMINOLOGY WARNING (registry_hierarchy 'boundaries', DISTINCT): the P0.2 sense = tree-node extents (TreeNode.section_range) + parent/child chunk extents — NOT Ghost A child_boundaries (that is L367's P0.1 sense). Also 'parent/child' here: rollup membership is parent_ids (member parent-chunk ids) vs tree structure child_node_ids — assert both without conflating. YT-transcript and table/code lanes already have exemplary chunker handling (chunker-spectrum verdict), so transcript fixtures should pass today.
required_receipt: Fixture test file(s) in repo, run GREEN in the container (exit 0), covering all 5 formats with golden assertions on profile/section/rollup/parent/child extents; L400's page-heading case included.

verbatim: [ ] Existing trees use the passthrough path without re-embedding.
action: Probe that the passthrough backfill was payload-only: for a sample of backfilled one-child sections, fetch the Qdrant point vectors before/after references (backups hold pre-state) and assert vectors are unchanged; confirm query-time descent takes the passthrough branch for those sections (navigator diagnostics).
where: Qdrant corpus_{cid8}_schemas points; backfill backups; backend/services/retriever/summary_tree_navigator.py diagnostics
why: Re-embedding 20k sections would have been an unbudgeted paid pass; proving payload-only repair certifies the migration's cheapness claim.
feasibility_note: Gap-filler. backfill_passthrough_sections.py wrote backups (ledger receipt), giving pre-state for the identity check; set_payload-style updates leave vectors untouched by construction, so this is a cheap confirmation rather than a risky investigation.
required_receipt: Probe output: N sampled backfilled sections, vector checksum identical pre/post, passthrough branch taken on live descent for each; exit 0.

verbatim: [ ] One-child descent performs no redundant vector search.
action: Instrumented replay: run a fixed query set that routes through one-child sections and assert (via navigator search-count diagnostics or Qdrant request logs) that zero rollup vector searches are issued where passthrough_hits applies.
where: backend/services/retriever/summary_tree_navigator.py:232-290; Qdrant search logs / lane diagnostics ('descent' labels)
why: This is the acceptance that converts L385's code into a measured latency/round-trip win.
feasibility_note: Gap-filler; the skip logic and its diagnostics exist (registry_hierarchy 'passthrough' + 'hierarchy descent' rows: lane diagnostics label descent modes). Shares its replay harness with L390(b) — build once, close both.
required_receipt: Replay report: per-query search counts with 0 redundant rollup searches on passthrough sections; exit-0 assertion; ledger restamp.

verbatim: [ ] Broad retrieval still reaches every parent formerly reachable through the duplicate section.
action: Reachability parity probe: for each backfilled one-child section, assert the union of parents reachable via the passthrough path equals the parent set formerly reachable through the duplicate rollup (compare passthrough_parent_ids against the rollup node's parent_ids); then a broad-retrieval smoke over sampled queries confirming no parent disappears from results.
where: Mongo summary_tree (passthrough_parent_ids vs rollup parent_ids); retriever broad path (Funnel A / tree descent)
why: The shortcut must be an optimization, not a recall hole — every formerly reachable parent must stay reachable.
feasibility_note: Gap-filler; purely deterministic set comparison plus a smoke run — both id sets are durable fields (registry_hierarchy 'passthrough'). Reminder from the 'parent' collision note: both sides are parent-CHUNK id lists, not tree ancestry; compare like with like.
required_receipt: Parity report: 100% of one-child sections with passthrough_parent_ids == rollup parent_ids (set equality), 0 recall-lost parents in the smoke set; exit 0.

verbatim: [ ] New trees do not fragment at page-number headings.
action: Include a page-number-heading fixture in the L391 suite: a document whose parser output contains 'Page N' headings mid-flow; assert the built tree contains no section keyed on a Page-N heading and that affected parents inherit the last real heading (per _is_page_heading behavior).
where: backend/tests (fixture); backend/services/ingestion/summary_tree.py:288-300
why: Behavioral proof of L383 — prevents regeneration of the degenerate one-child-section pathology on every future ingest.
feasibility_note: Directly testable against shipped code (_is_page_heading, _PAGE_HEADING_RE); zero data-state dependencies. Cheapest of the four P0.2 acceptances — fold into L391's suite rather than a separate harness. Relevant to S3's reingest decision: if ecom reingests, this test is the guard that new trees come out clean.
required_receipt: Fixture test GREEN: tree built from the Page-N fixture has 0 sections with page-label headings and expected section count; runs in the container, exit 0.
